proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e=1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e=0`.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=0`.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast: mean(dependency_BRAF | braf_v600e=1) - mean(dependency_BRAF | braf_v600e=0).
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1` lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8047 (n=5574) versus -0.2495 in `braf_v600e=0` lines; BRAF V600E was the dominant BRAF-dependency signal.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast: mean(dependency_EGFR | egfr_amplification=1) - mean(dependency_EGFR | egfr_amplification=0).
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6010 (n=3880) versus -0.2524 without EGFR amplification.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast: mean(dependency_ERBB2 | erbb2_amplification=1) - mean(dependency_ERBB2 | erbb2_amplification=0).
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.6856 (n=4890) versus -0.2535 without ERBB2 amplification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation=0`.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss=1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss=0`, consistent with RAS-pathway dependency.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast: mean(dependency_KRAS | kras_mutation=1) - mean(dependency_KRAS | kras_mutation=0).
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5594 (n=11842) versus -0.2520 in KRAS-wild-type lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast: mean(dependency_RIT1 | nf1_loss=1) - mean(dependency_RIT1 | nf1_loss=0).
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.3527 (n=4560) versus -0.2572 in `nf1_loss=0` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964, the 75th percentile) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing lines.
Cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` (>=0.964, the 75th percentile) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing lines.
Cell lines with high `cas9_activity_score` (>=1.080, the 75th percentile) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than lower-Cas9 lines, suggesting a screen-quality component.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 75th percentile of growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 versus -0.3928 in the rest of the dataset.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 75th percentile of growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 versus -0.2447 in the rest of the dataset.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 75th percentile of cas9_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: High-Cas9 lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2693 versus -0.2488 in lower-Cas9 lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `copy_number_8q24` (>=0.428, the 75th percentile) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines below that threshold.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than lines with `myc_amplification=0`.
Cell lines with higher `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines with lower MYC expression.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 75th percentile of copy_number_8q24.
analyses.result_summary: High-8q24 lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3440 versus -0.2452 in lower-8q24 lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style two-group contrast for myc_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification=1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 versus -0.2523 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Pearson/simple-linear slope of dependency_DHX9 on rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` became more negative as `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` increased; slope was -0.0161 dependency units per log2 TPM.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `lineage='colorectal'` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation=0`.
Cell lines with high `wnt_activity_score` (>=1.427, the 90th percentile) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-WNT lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style contrast: colorectal lineage versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 (n=5816) versus -0.2524 in non-colorectal lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style contrast for apc_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation=1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 versus -0.2526 in `apc_mutation=0` lines.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 90th percentile of wnt_activity_score.
analyses.result_summary: WNT-high lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.6463 versus -0.2690 in lower-WNT lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `lineage='colorectal'` cell lines with `wnt_activity_score>=1.427` and `smad4_loss=0` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Within `lineage='colorectal'` and `wnt_activity_score>=1.427` cell lines, `smad4_loss=0` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than `smad4_loss=1` cell lines.
`lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Three-predicate subgroup contrast: colorectal AND WNT90 AND SMAD4-intact versus complement.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/WNT90/SMAD4-intact subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2409 (n=2012) versus -0.2677 in the complement.

analyses.code: Within colorectal WNT90 lines, contrast smad4_loss=0 versus smad4_loss=1.
analyses.result_summary: Among colorectal WNT90 lines, `smad4_loss=0` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2409 (n=2012) versus -0.2642 for `smad4_loss=1` (n=442), indicating SMAD4 loss suppresses the dependency.

analyses.code: Four-predicate subgroup contrast: colorectal AND APC-mutant AND WNT75 AND SMAD4-intact versus complement.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT75/SMAD4-intact subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 (n=2571) versus -0.2530 in the complement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_BRAF`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is `braf_v600e=1`, with more negative `dependency_BRAF` than the complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_EGFR`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is `egfr_amplification=1`, with more negative `dependency_EGFR` than the complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_ERBB2`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is `erbb2_amplification=1`, with more negative `dependency_ERBB2` than the complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KRAS`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is `kras_mutation=1`, with more negative `dependency_KRAS` than the complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RIT1`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is `nf1_loss=1`, with more negative `dependency_RIT1` than the complement.

analyses.code: Systematic feature and small-subgroup screen over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates for dependency_BRAF.
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e=1` remained the top single feature. A `braf_v600e=1 & lineage=skin` subgroup was also strong (mean -0.8114, n=1966), but the dependency was already present across BRAF-mutant lines.

analyses.code: Systematic feature and small-subgroup screen over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates for dependency_EGFR.
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification=1` remained the top single feature. A `egfr_amplification=1 & lineage=brain` subgroup had mean -0.6117, but the broad EGFR-amplified subgroup captured the signal.

analyses.code: Systematic feature and small-subgroup screen over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates for dependency_ERBB2.
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification=1` remained the top single feature. Breast and MSI-negative intersections were also strong, but ERBB2 amplification was the complete driver.

analyses.code: Systematic feature and small-subgroup screen over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates for dependency_KRAS.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation=1` remained the top feature. Pancreatic and colorectal lineage enrichment was visible, but KRAS mutation explained the dependency direction most directly.

analyses.code: Systematic feature and small-subgroup screen over binary, categorical, and quantile-threshold predicates for dependency_RIT1.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss=1` remained the top feature. Pairing NF1 loss with `cdkn2a_loss=0` and `brca2_loss=0` slightly sharpened the mean (-0.3574), but the primary direction was NF1 loss.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_KIF18A`, the strongest complete subgroup is `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, `smad4_loss=0`, and high `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm>=3.559`, with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` than all other lines.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_TMED10`, cell lines with high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` and high `cas9_activity_score` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than the rest, while no stronger cancer-genomic subgroup is evident.

analyses.code: Five-predicate subgroup contrast: colorectal AND APC-mutant AND WNT75 AND SMAD4-intact AND ASCL2-high versus complement.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT75/SMAD4-intact/ASCL2-high subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.3320 (n=1511) versus -0.2749 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Systematic small-subgroup screen for dependency_TMED10; top compound screen-covariate subgroup was high growth plus high Cas9.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=1.088` and `cas9_activity_score>=1.080` lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.3130 (n=1264) versus -0.2525 in the complement; molecular predicates were weaker and less coherent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_DHX9`, cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm>=5.669` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than the complement.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_POLR2A`, cell lines with very high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=1.088` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than the complement.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for `dependency_RPL11`, cell lines with high `copy_number_8q24>=0.428` and `myc_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than the complement.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup contrast for dependency_DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth/MYC-high lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3892 (n=3320) versus -0.3297 in the complement.

analyses.code: Threshold contrast at the 90th percentile of growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: Very high-growth lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.5281 (n=5002) versus -0.4070 in the complement.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup contrast for dependency_RPL11.
analyses.result_summary: High-8q24/MYC-amplified lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3647 (n=7797) versus -0.2524 in the complement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final best-supported buried dependency subgroup is `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, `smad4_loss=0`, and `rnaseq_ASCL2_log2_tpm>=3.559`, which has markedly more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Within `lineage='colorectal'`, `apc_mutation=1`, and `wnt_activity_score>=0.819` cell lines, `smad4_loss=0` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than `smad4_loss=1` cell lines.

For `dependency_TMED10`, the best-supported pattern is a modest screen/proliferation association: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=0.964` lines have more negative scores than lower-growth lines, with no strong lineage or genomic-feature subgroup comparable to the KIF18A subgroup.

analyses.code: Final five-predicate KIF18A subgroup contrast.
analyses.result_summary: The final colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT75/SMAD4-intact/ASCL2-high subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.3320 (n=1511) versus -0.2749 in the complement.

analyses.code: Within colorectal APC-mutant WNT75 lines, contrast smad4_loss=0 versus smad4_loss=1.
analyses.result_summary: Within colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT75 lines, `smad4_loss=0` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 (n=2571) versus -0.2557 for `smad4_loss=1` (n=562).

analyses.code: Systematic dependency_TMED10 search over single, pair, and triple predicates.
analyses.result_summary: The single strongest broad `dependency_TMED10` pattern was high growth (mean -0.2816 versus -0.2447; effect -0.0369). Compound high-growth/high-Cas9 rules sharpened this modestly, but no coherent molecular subgroup emerged.
